title: Low water tank level and supply uncertainty
summary: The main irrigation water tank is approaching a critically low level while supply continuity is uncertain during active production conditions.
suspected_issue: The greenhouse may soon be unable to complete planned irrigation cycles because water reserves are low and incoming supply is uncertain.
symptoms: The main tank level is approaching a critical minimum threshold.
Planned irrigation demand may exceed available reserves if the current situation continues.
Incoming replenishment is uncertain and cannot be assumed as guaranteed.
The crop is in production phase and delayed irrigation may create stress and yield-quality risk.

observed_signals.interpretation: Tank level is critically low for normal production continuity.
The current flow is still functional, which means the issue is reserve uncertainty rather than an immediate mechanical outage.
Pressure is currently normal, so the main concern is near-term supply continuity rather than pressure failure.

operational_context.note: The greenhouse remains in active production and irrigation demand is still expected during the next cycles.
operational_context.impact: The service should not treat this as a harmless warning because production irrigation still has to be executed.

operational_context.note: No guaranteed backup water reserve is confirmed for this situation.
operational_context.impact: The service should emphasize prioritization, review, and near-term action rather than assuming easy continuity.

agronomic_context.note: Production-stage tomato crop may experience stress quickly if irrigation cycles are reduced or delayed.
agronomic_context.impact: A poor decision here may affect both plant health and commercial quality.

business_context.note: Water reserve uncertainty directly threatens continuity of normal production operations.
business_context.impact: The business risk comes from interruption of irrigation service before the next safe replenishment window.

notes: This case is designed to test whether Agro-DO responds appropriately when the main risk is not an immediate equipment failure, but a near-term inability to sustain irrigation continuity because reserves are running low.
